workflow_rationale: Multi-service feature requiring backend schema changes, OAuth integrations, API sync jobs, and frontend analytics dashboard. Phases follow dependency order: database → integrations → sync → UI → integration.

phases.name: Database Schema for Social Analytics
phases.description: Add database tables for Twitter and LinkedIn integrations, social post publications, and analytics metrics snapshots

subtasks.description: Add social integration tables (twitterIntegrations, linkedinIntegrations) to schema.ts
subtasks.notes: Added twitterIntegrations and linkedinIntegrations tables to schema.ts following devtoIntegrations pattern. Both tables include OAuth fields (accessToken, refreshToken, accessTokenExpiresAt), platform user ID, username, enabled flag, and lastSyncAt. Added relations and updated workspacesRelations. Also resolved pre-existing merge conflict in 0000_snapshot.json. Migration generated successfully as 0002_faithful_landau.sql.

subtasks.description: Add social publications tables (twitterPublications, linkedinPublications) to track posted content
subtasks.notes: Added twitterPublications and linkedinPublications tables following the devtoPublications pattern. Updated workspace, post, twitter/linkedin integration relations to include the new publication tables. Migration generated successfully (0003_dizzy_forge.sql).

subtasks.description: Add socialAnalytics table to store engagement metrics snapshots
subtasks.notes: Added socialPlatformEnum (twitter/linkedin) and socialAnalytics table with workspaceId, postId, platform, impressions, likes, shares, comments, clicks, rawMetrics, syncedAt fields. Includes indexes on workspaceId, postId, platform, syncedAt, and a unique constraint on (postId, platform). Added socialAnalyticsRelations and updated workspacesRelations and postsRelations. Migration generated successfully as 0004_nifty_mimic.sql.

subtasks.description: Add relations for new social tables and push schema to database
subtasks.notes: Relations for all social tables were already present in schema.ts from prior subtasks (1-1 through 1-3). Schema pushed to Neon database: created social_platform enum and all 5 social tables (twitter_integrations, linkedin_integrations, twitter_publications, linkedin_publications, social_analytics) with all correct indexes and foreign keys. Used targeted psql SQL to safely push against the shared database without dropping unrelated tables from other worktrees.

phases.name: Twitter/X OAuth Integration
phases.description: Implement Twitter OAuth 2.0 flow, API client, and integration settings UI

subtasks.description: Create Twitter API client with OAuth 2.0 and analytics endpoints
subtasks.notes: Created apps/dashboard/src/lib/integrations/twitter.ts following the devto.ts pattern. Uses native fetch against Twitter API v2 (no external packages needed). Implements: verifyTwitterAuth (verifies OAuth 2.0 Bearer token via /users/me), getTwitterUser (alias for verifyTwitterAuth), getTweetAnalytics (fetches paginated user tweets with public_metrics), and getTweetById. Maps all required metrics: impressions, likes, retweets, replies, clicks, quotes. Error handling with TwitterApiError classifying unauthorized/forbidden/not_found/rate_limited/unknown. Also fixed pre-existing bug in schema.ts where postsRelations destructured only `one` but called `many(socialAnalytics)`. Build passes successfully.

subtasks.description: Create Twitter OAuth initiation route (/api/integrations/twitter/oauth)
subtasks.notes: Created GET /api/integrations/twitter/oauth route implementing Twitter OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow. Validates session and workspace ownership, generates cryptographically secure state and code_verifier, computes SHA-256 code_challenge, stores all in httpOnly cookies (10min TTL), and returns 302 redirect to Twitter authorization URL. Build verified successfully.

subtasks.description: Create Twitter OAuth callback route (/api/integrations/twitter/callback)

verification.instructions: Complete OAuth flow end-to-end; verify tokens stored in twitterIntegrations table

subtasks.notes: Created Twitter OAuth callback route at apps/dashboard/src/app/api/integrations/twitter/callback/route.ts. Handles PKCE OAuth 2.0 flow: validates state cookie, exchanges code for tokens, verifies Twitter user via API, stores credentials in twitterIntegrations table with upsert, clears OAuth cookies. TypeScript compiles cleanly.

subtasks.description: Create Twitter integration management route (GET/DELETE /api/integrations/twitter)
subtasks.notes: Created GET/DELETE route at apps/dashboard/src/app/api/integrations/twitter/route.ts following the devto pattern. GET returns connection status with username, twitterUserId, enabled, lastSyncAt, connectedAt. DELETE removes the integration. Both endpoints verify auth and workspace ownership.

subtasks.description: Add Twitter integration UI to integrations settings page
subtasks.notes: Added Twitter/X integration card below the Dev.to card on the integrations settings page. Uses useQuery to fetch status from GET /api/integrations/twitter, renders connected state with username and connectedAt timestamp, provides OAuth redirect link to /api/integrations/twitter/oauth for connecting, and disconnect button using DELETE /api/integrations/twitter. Wrapped both cards in a space-y-4 div for clean layout.

phases.name: LinkedIn OAuth Integration
phases.description: Implement LinkedIn OAuth 2.0 flow, API client, and integration settings UI

subtasks.description: Create LinkedIn API client with OAuth 2.0 and analytics endpoints
subtasks.notes: Created apps/dashboard/src/lib/integrations/linkedin.ts following Twitter/devto patterns. Uses native fetch against LinkedIn API v2 with Bearer token auth and X-Restli-Protocol-Version header. Implements: verifyLinkedInAuth (via /userinfo endpoint), getLinkedInUser (alias), getLinkedInPostAnalytics (paginated posts with engagement stats, graceful fallback when analytics endpoint is inaccessible), getLinkedInPostById. Error handling with LinkedInApiError classifying unauthorized/forbidden/not_found/rate_limited/unknown. Build verified successfully.

subtasks.description: Create LinkedIn OAuth initiation route (/api/integrations/linkedin/oauth)
subtasks.notes: Created LinkedIn OAuth initiation route at apps/dashboard/src/app/api/integrations/linkedin/oauth/route.ts following the Twitter OAuth pattern. Uses state cookie for CSRF protection, redirects to LinkedIn authorization URL with openid/profile/email/w_member_social/r_basicprofile scopes.

subtasks.description: Create LinkedIn OAuth callback route (/api/integrations/linkedin/callback)

verification.instructions: Complete OAuth flow end-to-end; verify tokens stored in linkedinIntegrations table

subtasks.notes: Created LinkedIn OAuth callback route at apps/dashboard/src/app/api/integrations/linkedin/callback/route.ts. Follows Twitter callback pattern: validates state cookie, exchanges code for tokens (using client_secret in body per LinkedIn's OAuth spec, not PKCE), verifies LinkedIn user via verifyLinkedInAuth, and upserts into linkedinIntegrations table. Clears OAuth cookies on success.

subtasks.description: Create LinkedIn integration management route (GET/DELETE /api/integrations/linkedin)
subtasks.notes: Created LinkedIn integration management route following the Twitter pattern. GET returns connection status with username/linkedinUserId/enabled/lastSyncAt/connectedAt fields. DELETE removes the integration. Both handlers include auth check, workspace validation, and proper error responses.

subtasks.description: Add LinkedIn integration UI to integrations settings page
subtasks.notes: Added LinkedIn integration card to integrations settings page following the Twitter OAuth pattern. Includes useQuery for status, useMutation for disconnect, connected/not-connected states, OAuth redirect link, and error handling.

phases.name: Analytics Data Sync Service
phases.description: Build background sync service to pull engagement metrics from Twitter and LinkedIn APIs

subtasks.description: Create analytics sync API route to pull metrics for all connected platforms
subtasks.notes: Created POST /api/analytics/social/sync route. Fetches tweet analytics from Twitter API and LinkedIn post analytics, matches them against twitterPublications/linkedinPublications to find postIds, upserts into socialAnalytics table, updates lastSyncAt on integrations. Build verified successfully.

subtasks.description: Add sync trigger configuration to automation system (manual + scheduled)

checks: Can create social analytics sync trigger

subtasks.description: Create QStash webhook handler for scheduled analytics sync

verification.instructions: Trigger QStash job manually via automation page; verify metrics are synced

phases.name: Analytics Dashboard UI
phases.description: Build analytics dashboard with metrics cards, trend charts, and cross-platform comparison views

subtasks.description: Create analytics API route to fetch aggregated metrics
subtasks.notes: Support query params: workspace, timeframe (7d/30d/90d), platform filter

subtasks.description: Create analytics dashboard page with metrics overview
Create reusable MetricsCard component for individual metric display

checks: Metrics cards display impressions, likes, shares, comments

subtasks.description: Create TrendChart component for time-series visualization
subtasks.notes: Consider using recharts or similar library for visualization

subtasks.description: Create PlatformComparison component for cross-platform analysis
Add analytics navigation link to workspace sidebar

phases.name: End-to-End Integration & Testing
phases.description: Wire all components together and verify complete analytics workflow

subtasks.description: End-to-end verification of Twitter analytics flow
End-to-end verification of LinkedIn analytics flow
Verify cross-platform comparison view with real data

steps: Ensure both Twitter and LinkedIn are connected

subtasks.description: Error handling and edge case testing

verification.instructions: Test: expired OAuth tokens, API rate limits, missing posts, disconnected integrations, empty analytics state

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on phase-1-database, work on different files, can run concurrently

parallelism.speedup_estimate: ~25% faster than sequential (2 phases run in parallel)

acceptance_criteria: Users can connect Twitter/X accounts via OAuth
Users can connect LinkedIn accounts via OAuth
Per-post analytics show impressions, likes, shares, comments, clicks
No secrets in code or logs

verification_steps.name: OAuth Security Review
verification_steps.expected_outcome: OAuth tokens encrypted, PKCE used, redirect URIs validated

verification_strategy.reasoning: High risk due to OAuth implementation, sensitive token storage, and external API dependencies. Requires security scanning and OAuth best practices review.
